summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn concluded the True Love series with a powerful message on the boundless nature of God's love and grace. He opened by declaring the foundational truth that nothing we can do—no sin, failure, or hidden habit—can make God stop loving us. This unconditional, covenant love was demonstrated supremely when Christ died for us while we were still sinners, as stated in Romans 5:8. The sermon centered on the promise from Romans 8:38-39 that nothing in all creation can separate us from God's love. Pastor Travis then transitioned to explore the practical implications of this love through the lens of God's grace, which is always greater than our human condition. He presented three key truths: God's grace is greater than our sin, greater than our shame, and greater than our ongoing struggles. Using personal stories, including his own journey of sanctification and recovery from a stroke, and biblical examples like the woman caught in adultery, he illustrated that grace does not ignore guilt but covers it, transforming our identity from 'mistake' to 'child of God.' The message emphasized that believers are in a process, and we should 'praise the progress,' trusting that God's ocean of grace swallows up our sin. The call to action invited those who had never committed their lives to Jesus, or who had drifted away, to make a decision to follow or recommit to Him, resting in the truth that His goodness and mercy follow us all our days.
mainPoints: God's unconditional love is a covenant reality; nothing we do can separate us from it, as demonstrated by Christ's death for us while we were still sinners.
God's grace is greater than our sin, covering all our failures, and greater than our shame, removing condemnation and redefining our identity in Christ.
God's grace is greater than our present struggles and ongoing battles, providing the power to fight from a position of grace, not for it, as we undergo sanctification.

versesMentioned.text: But God demonstrates his own love for us in this: While we were still sinners, Christ died for us.

For I am convinced that neither death nor life, neither angels nor demons, neither the present nor the future, nor any powers, neither height nor depth, nor anything else in all creation, will be able to separate us from the love of God that is in Christ Jesus our Lord.
See what great love the Father has lavished on us, that we should be called children of God! And that is what we are!
for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God,
The law was brought in so that the trespass might increase. But where sin increased, grace increased all the more,
God made him who had no sin to be sin for us, so that in him we might become the righteousness of God.
For the wages of sin is death, but the gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.
For God did not send his Son into the world to condemn the world, but to save the world through him.
Therefore, there is now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus,
You will again have compassion on us; you will tread our sins underfoot and hurl all our iniquities into the depths of the sea.

I do not understand what I do. For what I want to do I do not do, but what I hate I do. And if I do what I do not want to do, I agree that the law is good. As it is, it is no longer I myself who do it, but it is sin living in me. For I know that good itself does not dwell in me, that is, in my sinful nature. For I have the desire to do what is good, but I cannot carry it out. For I do not do the good I want to do, but the evil I do not want to do—this I keep on doing.

So I find this law at work: Although I want to do good, evil is right there with me. For in my inner being I delight in God’s law; but I see another law at work in me, waging war against the law of my mind and making me a prisoner of the law of sin at work within me. What a wretched man I am! Who will rescue me from this body that is subject to death? Thanks be to God, who delivers me through Jesus Christ our Lord!
If you declare with your mouth, “Jesus is Lord,” and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
Surely your goodness and love will follow me all the days of my life, and I will dwell in the house of the LORD forever.

Monday.scripture: Romans 5:8 (NIV) - But God demonstrates his own love for us in this: While we were still sinners, Christ died for us.
Monday.focus: The unconditional nature of God's love.
Monday.action: Reflect on a past mistake and consciously receive God's love for you in that moment, as if it were happening now.
Monday.prayer: God, thank you that your love for me is not based on my performance. Help me to truly believe and rest in your unconditional love today.

Tuesday.scripture: Romans 8:1 (NIV) - Therefore, there is now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.
Tuesday.action: Identify one thing you feel ashamed of. Write it down, then physically tear up the paper as a symbol of giving it to God's grace.
Tuesday.prayer: Lord, I receive your truth that there is no condemnation for me in Christ. Please silence the voice of shame and help me walk in your freedom.

Wednesday.scripture: Micah 7:19 (NIV) - You will again have compassion on us; you will tread our sins underfoot and hurl all our iniquities into the depths of the sea.
Wednesday.action: When a past failure comes to mind today, consciously say, 'That is in the sea. I will not fish for it.'
Wednesday.prayer: Father, thank you for hurling my sins into the sea. Help me to leave them there and to live in the newness of life you provide.

Thursday.scripture: Romans 7:24-25 (NIV) - What a wretched man I am! Who will rescue me...? Thanks be to God, who delivers me through Jesus Christ our Lord!
Thursday.focus: Grace in the midst of ongoing struggle.
Thursday.action: Acknowledge a current struggle without judgment. Tell a trusted friend or write in a journal, 'I'm struggling with X, but God's grace is greater.'
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, I thank you that you are my rescuer. In my weakness and struggle, I rely on your delivering grace. Strengthen me to fight from grace, not for it.

Friday.scripture: 1 John 3:1 (NIV) - See what great love the Father has lavished on us, that we should be called children of God! And that is what we are!
Friday.focus: Our secure identity as God's children.
Friday.action: Look at yourself in a mirror and say out loud, 'I am a loved child of God.'
Friday.prayer: Heavenly Father, I am in awe that you call me your child. Help this truth to sink deep into my heart and shape how I see myself and interact with others.

Saturday.scripture: Psalm 23:6 (NIV) - Surely your goodness and love will follow me all the days of my life, and I will dwell in the house of the LORD forever.
Saturday.action: Review your week. Identify one instance where you experienced God's goodness or mercy, even in a small way, and thank Him for it.
Saturday.prayer: Lord, thank you that your goodness and mercy follow me every single day. I trust you to lead me and clean up my messes. My heart is your home.
